parsed_review.summary: CP00-279 is a descriptor-only Risk-Class-A pack covering the 10 RP08.P02.M07 ("Test And Golden Case Set") middle rows RP08.P02.M07.S03-S12 (tenant/matter/permission/audit prechecks, primary/secondary paths, state-transition enforcement, idempotency-key handling, lock-acquisition rule, persistence boundary). Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob only) confirms the production_ready_candidate claim is supported and consistent across plan -> baseline -> manifest -> registry binding -> service descriptor -> contract projection -> validator script -> unit tests. Coverage matches exactly: 10 units, deliverable split 6 implementation / 2 security_audit / 2 ui, single M07 section of 10 rows. No-write / no-real-data is enforced both structurally (service.js freezeCp279Result freezes every dispatch/write/persist/storage/leak flag to false and synthetic_only/no_real_data to true) and by validation (validators.js enforces per-row descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false, plus the security-relevant rows cross_tenant_access_allowed=false, matter_trace_required=true, deny_over_allow_enforced=true, audit_hint_detail_included=false, writes_state_transition=false, persists_idempotency_key=false, acquires_runtime_lock=false, creates_database_rows=false). The authority boundary holds at every layer: Claude is read_only and not final approver (descriptor.authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation (claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false), and human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; tests and the contract validator script assert these. Hermes is used only as a deterministic H08 evidence/receipt descriptor (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; no product runtime embedded), and the pack emits production_ready_candidate (coverage.valid && mid.valid), not a hard production_ready claim (manifest.production_ready=false, command-evidence.production_ready=false). CP178+ review-hardening evidence is valid: auth_preflight passed with non-zero stdout (1248 bytes) and well-formed JSON, prompt 2135 bytes < 25600, source_inspection_mode=read_tools_used (summary-only correctly prohibited due to security/audit deliverables), tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob with forbidden_tools_present=false. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. Two P3 findings are transparency notes inherent to a read-only review of an implemented_pending_review pack and do not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: Validation-command exit codes are attested in command-evidence, not independently executed under read-only review
findings.details: command-evidence.json records 20 commands (node --check/--test, npm run rp08:email-dms-core:validate, closeout-pack-plan:validate, weighted/spec/rp00-control-plane/goal-closeout/fullplan:validate, npm test/validate/build, git diff --check) all with exit_code 0, plus Hermes gate H08 passed. As a read-only reviewer restricted to Read/Grep/Glob (no Bash), I cannot re-execute these to confirm the exit codes first-hand; the green status is taken from the evidence file. This is a transparency limitation of the read-only review model, not an observed defect.
findings.recommendation: Continue to rely on the deterministic harness (readiness auth-preflight, raw-output capture, receipt-validate, construction-inspection) to bind asserted command results to the committed receipt; no pack change required. Mitigated here by independent source-level verification that the invariants those commands check (coverage counts, descriptor-only flags, authority boundary, contract deepEqual) actually hold in the source.

findings.title: Manifest evidence_refs reference post-review artifacts not yet present at review time
findings.details: manifest.json evidence_refs and required_pack_artifacts list docs/closeout-packs/cp00-279/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, which are produced only after this review is accepted and normalized. At review time the pack is status implemented_pending_review and pack_level_claude_review.status is pending, so these artifacts are expected to be absent. This is the normal pending-review state, not a missing-evidence defect.
findings.recommendation: No action required; the hardening sequence (normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation -> commit) generates these artifacts before commit. Confirm they exist and are non-empty at final_closeout_validation prior to committing CP00-279.
